mechanism: Yield curve inversion predicts recessions (7/7 record) which drive equity drawdowns. However, the signal requires decomposition: inversion from expected-rate compression signals recession, while inversion from term premium compression may not. The steepening trap means recession arrives during steepening, not inversion.
regime_dependence: Decomposition requirement is heightened in QT era where term premium behavior differs from pre-2008. ACM and Kim-Wright models diverge by 30-50bp, introducing model dependence.
